name: The Police Credit Union Irving Street Branch
description_short: Credit union serving law enforcement officers and their communities with auto loans, mortgages, home improvement financing, and member benefits including debt forgiveness programs.
description_long:
The Police Credit Union (SFPCU) is a specialized financial institution designed specifically for law enforcement professionals and their support networks. Founded to serve the unique needs of the law enforcement community, the credit union has built a membership base that includes active officers, retired law enforcement, and community supporters. The organization operates multiple branches, including the Irving Street location, and provides comprehensive financial services through both in-person and virtual channels. The Police Credit Union maintains a website at thepolicecu.org and operates a virtual branch during extended hours to serve members across different time zones.

The Police Credit Union offers a broad range of financial products tailored to law enforcement personnel and their families. Auto loans start at rates as low as 4.94% APR with flexible terms up to 84 months, including special promotions with Enterprise Car Sales offering additional discounts. Home loans include traditional fixed and variable rate mortgages alongside specialized H.E.R.O. loans featuring 0% down payments exclusively for active law enforcement first-time homebuyers. The institution provides home improvement loans up to $75,000 at rates as low as 8.24% APR, green energy loans up to $75,000, and credit cards (Platinum Visa) starting at 12.75% APR. Additional specialized products include the 10-Eight Loan for new law enforcement employees purchasing equipment and supplies, and an End of Watch debt forgiveness program providing coverage up to $850,000 for members' loved ones.

What distinguishes The Police Credit Union from traditional banks is its exclusive focus on the law enforcement community and purpose-built products addressing officer-specific financial needs. The H.E.R.O. loan program with zero down payments and the End of Watch debt forgiveness initiative represent targeted solutions not widely available from conventional lenders. The institution offers member scholarships ($2,500 annually, five awarded), special member discounts, and employment-specific products like the 10-Eight Loan. Member testimonials highlight competitive rates compared to traditional banking alternatives and professional customer service both in-person and by phone (800.222.1391). The virtual branch extends accessibility with hours Monday-Friday 10am-5pm PST and Saturday 9am-3pm PST.

The Police Credit Union operates as a legitimate member-focused financial institution with transparent rate disclosures and standard lending terms. However, membership eligibility appears restricted to law enforcement professionals and affiliated individuals, limiting access for general consumers. Rate qualifications explicitly require "excellent borrower history," meaning advertised rates represent best-case scenarios. Terms include standard property limitations (home improvement loans for primary residence only) and maximum vehicle mileage restrictions (175,000 miles). The institution's specialized focus, while beneficial for its target market, means services are not universally accessible to all consumers seeking credit union banking services.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Specialized H.E.R.O. mortgages with 0% down payment for active law enforcement first-time homebuyers
End of Watch debt forgiveness program up to $850,000 to protect members' families
Auto loan rates as low as 4.94% APR with Enterprise Car Sales promotion offering additional 1% discount
10-Eight Loan specifically designed for new law enforcement employees to purchase required equipment and supplies
Extended virtual branch hours (Monday-Friday 10am-5pm, Saturday 9am-3pm PST) for remote accessibility
Annual scholarship program awarding five $2,500 scholarships to deserving member students
Competitive rates on home improvement loans up to $75,000 and green energy loans up to $75,000
cons: Membership eligibility restricted to law enforcement professionals and affiliated individuals, not available to general consumers
Advertised rates require 'excellent borrower history' — actual APR likely higher for average credit profiles
Home improvement loans limited to one per membership/household with primary residence-only property restrictions
Auto loans with 73-84 month terms require minimum $25,000 loan amount, excluding smaller vehicle purchases
Limited branch network visibility — specific Irving Street location details not provided on main website
best_for: Active and retired law enforcement officers seeking specialized loan products designed for their profession
Law enforcement employees needing equipment financing through the 10-Eight Loan program
First-time homebuyers in law enforcement pursuing H.E.R.O. mortgages with zero down payment
Officers and families seeking debt protection through End of Watch forgiveness programs
diagnosis: The Police Credit Union is best for active and retired law enforcement professionals seeking specialized financial products with favorable terms designed for their profession, particularly those interested in homebuying assistance or equipment financing. The primary caveat is that membership eligibility is restricted to law enforcement and affiliated individuals — this is not a credit union available to general consumers — and advertised rates require excellent credit history, meaning actual rates will likely be higher for borrowers with average or fair credit profiles.
services: Auto loans (new and used) with rates as low as 4.94% APR and terms up to 84 months
Home loans including fixed and variable rate mortgages
H.E.R.O. mortgages with 0% down payment for active law enforcement first-time homebuyers
Home improvement loans up to $75,000 at rates as low as 8.24% APR
Green energy loans up to $75,000 for renewable energy purchases
Platinum Visa credit cards with rates as low as 12.75% APR and rewards program
10-Eight Loan for new law enforcement employees purchasing supplies and equipment
End of Watch debt forgiveness program up to $850,000
Special member discounts and promotional offers
Virtual branch services with extended hours
Savings accounts with $25 minimum opening deposit including teen and youth accounts
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
